title: Technology Acceptable Use ‚Äî USB Mass Storage on CDE Workstations

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 2.2.4 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 2.2.4 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.89: Technology Acceptable Use ‚Äî USB Mass Storage on CDE Workstations.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.89' running on index=windows Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration or WinEventLog:Security EventCode 6416, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration" earliest=-7d
| where match(ClassName,"(?i)USBSTOR|diskdrive)")
| lookup pci_cde_workstations.csv ComputerName OUTPUT in_cde_ws
| where in_cde_ws="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_insert, values(DeviceDescription) as dev by ComputerName, InstanceId
| sort - first_insert
description: Surfaces device plug events for removable media on in-scope workstations so technology usage policies restricting untrusted storage near account data are enforced and auditable.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Enable PnP operational log forwarding on CDE laptops if any exist. (2) Block at GPO where possible; use this as detective control. (3) Investigate each event. (4) Map to acceptable use training module completion. Indexes required: `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration`; Lookups: `pci_cde_workstations`, `pci_cde_workstations.csv`.
visualization: Table (devices), timeline (first_insert), single value (weekly USB attempts).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=windows` `Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration` or `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 6416.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Enable PnP operational log forwarding on CDE laptops if any exist. (2) Block at GPO where possible; use this as detective control. (3) Investigate each event. (4) Map to acceptable use training module completion.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration" earliest=-7d
| where match(ClassName,"(?i)USBSTOR|diskdrive)")
| lookup pci_cde_workstations.csv ComputerName OUTPUT in_cde_ws
| where in_cde_ws="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_insert, values(DeviceDescription) as dev by ComputerName, InstanceId
| sort - first_insert
```

Understanding this SPL

**Technology Acceptable Use ‚Äî USB Mass Storage on CDE Workstations (PCI DSS Req 12.3.2, 2.2.4)** ‚Äî Surfaces device plug events for removable media on in-scope workstations so technology usage policies restricting untrusted storage near account data are enforced and auditable.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` `Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration` or `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 6416. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(ClassName,"(?i)USBSTOR|diskdrive)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where in_cde_ws="true"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ComputerName, InstanceId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Technology Acceptable Use ‚Äî USB Mass Storage on CDE Workstations (PCI DSS Req 12.3.2, 2.2.4)** ‚Äî Surfaces device plug events for removable media on in-scope workstations so technology usage policies restricting untrusted storage near account data are enforced and auditable.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` `Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration` or `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 6416. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Endpoint.Processes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (devices), timeline (first_insert), single value (weekly USB attempts).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for surfaces device plug events for removable media on in-scope workstations so technology usage policies restricting untrusted storage near account data are enforced and auditable. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
